summary.title: 抽出 Codex source adapter
summary.what: Moved the existing Codex parser and source inventory implementation behind a new src/sources adapter boundary, added a registry with codex as the only registered public adapter, routed sync/status through that adapter, and kept the old parser/source-inventory exports as compatibility facades.
summary.why: The session-source workflow needs an adapter boundary before later slices add source-aware storage, selector, coverage, and CLI source handling. I1 preserves current Codex behavior while giving I2/I3 a stable source abstraction to extend.

decisions.chose: Register only the public codex adapter in I1.
decisions.rationale: The workflow reserves claude-code but forbids implementing or advertising a Claude Code adapter in this slice.
rejected: Adding a Claude Code adapter stub with behavior
Changing public CLI source behavior before I3

decisions.chose: Keep src/parser.ts and src/source-inventory.ts as compatibility facades over the Codex adapter implementation files.
decisions.rationale: Current tests and callers keep importing the same modules while the implementation moves behind the adapter boundary.
rejected: Forcing all callers to switch to src/sources imports in I1

decisions.chose: Route sync/status through getSessionSourceAdapter("codex") but preserve strict snapshot options and existing selector shape.
decisions.rationale: I1 must not change DB schema, selector/coverage semantics, CLI flags, or Codex sync error behavior.
rejected: Adding source fields to selectors or storage in I1

rejected.alternative: Implement source-aware storage, selector, coverage, or CLI flags in the adapter slice.
rejected.reason: Those changes are explicitly assigned to I2/I3 and would exceed the I1 write boundary.

review_notes: Actual I1 commit is f5357c2661246d285224788d52f4875de40687f6; seal prepare also reported inherited workflow control-plane files because this detached worktree is based on prior workflow commits ahead of origin/main.
Validation passed: targeted parser/inventory/source/indexer/status tests, npm run check, git diff --check, and a checkout CLI status/sync/find smoke through npm run --silent cxs.
No DB schema, selector/coverage semantics, CLI flags, docs/skill, release, global install, push, PR, or Claude Code adapter implementation changed.

architectural_claims: Codex transcript discovery and parsing now have an explicit SessionSourceAdapter boundary.
The source registry currently exposes only the public codex adapter.
Legacy parser and source-inventory modules remain compatibility facades.
behavioral_changes: No intended user-visible behavior change; Codex remains the default and only active source.
syncSessions and collectStatus now call the Codex adapter for root resolution, inventory/snapshot collection, and parsing.

api_changes.signature: src/parser.ts and src/source-inventory.ts exports now forward to src/sources/codex-* implementations

security_implications: No new external input source is enabled; claude-code remains unregistered and non-public.
migration_notes: No database migration or index version bump in I1.
I2 must add source_id/native_session_id/session_key and source-aware selector/coverage/storage semantics.
